formulas.name: Debenture
formulas.formula: A type of security issued by a company to raise long-term funds.

formulas.name: Types of Debentures
formulas.notes: Secured debentures are backed by collateral, while unsecured debentures are not.

formulas.name: Features of a Debenture
formulas.formula: Face Value, Coupon Rate, Maturity Period, Redemption Value, Interest Rate
formulas.notes: These features are essential characteristics of a debenture.

formulas.name: Issue of Debentures
formulas.formula: Public Issue, Private Issue, Right Issue, Bonus Issue
formulas.notes: These are different methods of issuing debentures.

formulas.name: Redemption of Debentures
formulas.formula: At Face Value, At a Premium, At a Discount
formulas.notes: Debentures can be redeemed at their face value or at a premium or discount.

formulas.name: Redemption Fund
formulas.formula: A fund created by a company to meet the maturity of debentures.
formulas.notes: This fund is essential for the smooth redemption of debentures.

formulas.name: Sinking Fund
formulas.formula: A fund created by a company to meet the maturity of debentures through periodic installments.
formulas.notes: This fund helps in the systematic redemption of debentures.
